rows.notes: Clear centred page title at top of page, no monetary columns present on the same line.

rows.description: Dr. Shortridge's Benef: half a year to Midr.
rows.notes: Description legible. Pounds '100' is well-aligned under the pounds column; shillings and pence columns are blank/zero and alignment is unambiguous.
Amount reads as '08 0 0' in the pounds/shillings/pence columns. The leading zero on pounds visible; interpreted as 8 pounds. Text slightly cramped but digits are clear.
Numbers '50 0 0' clearly aligned in their columns; handwriting neat and unambiguous.

rows.description: Mids. & Xmas Div. in stock, 3 pr: cent: consol:
rows.notes: Description partly abbreviated but readable. Amount '15 8 4' clearly placed in the three narrow right-hand columns; pence '4' legible.
Amount '05 5 9' is well aligned; leading zero on pounds visible. Text of name 'Richards' readable; small flourish on digits but unambiguous.

rows.description: Took out of the Chest for L. Wittenham Div:
rows.notes: Large clear '200 0 0' in the pounds/shillings/pence columns; description legible. Alignment with vertical rules unambiguous.
Amount reads '01 1 0' clearly. The word 'Sconces' and abbreviation 'Disp:' are a bit uncertain (could be 'Sources' vs 'Sconces' or 'Diss.'), but the monetary columns are clear.

rows.description: Dr. Shortridge's Benef: half a year to Xmas
rows.notes: Very similar to earlier Shortridge entry; '100 0 0' clearly aligned and unambiguous.
Short left-hand month label marking the start of the June section; no monetary amount on this line.

rows.description: Mr. Oswald Jackson's Acct for the Merton estate for the year from Mic: 1773 to Mic: 1774
rows.notes: Centered account heading spanning several following lines. Handwriting readable though long; no amounts on this header line.
'205 - 0 - 0' is clearly written and perfectly aligned under the pounds/shillings/pence columns.

Amount reads '007 2 0' clearly, but the description word is less certain: the first letter looks like a 'G' or 'C' and I have transcribed it as 'Gork' to match the visible strokes. If a transcription of the name is required, this line should be rechecked against the original text.
Subtotal line (sum of Money Rent and Gork Rent) '272 2 0' is clearly written and aligned; recognized as a subtotal by its position and underline.
Line reads '24 - 17 - 3' and is clearly aligned in the three small columns; text 'Allowed Land Tax' is legible.

The main numeric triplet looks like '16 - 7 - 10'. There is a small extra mark after the pence that could be a notation (possibly a farthing or a ledger mark). Because that trailing symbol is unclear, I recorded pence as 10 with moderate confidence.

Line shows 'Deduct - 41 - 5 - 1' but there is a small additional mark after the pence (similar to the previous line). The main values 41, 5, 1 are legible; the extra trailing mark is ambiguous so pence fraction left blank.
Rightmost printed repetition shows '230 16 10½' clearly. The halfpenny symbol (loop like 'ob') after the pence is visible; interpreted as 0.5. Alignment and repetition increase confidence.

Amount appears as '55 10 2 3/4' (the pence column shows '2' followed by a '3q' or '3/4' style mark). The three-quarters symbol is visible and interpreted as 0.75; description reading 'Newbery' is legible though handwriting slightly flourished.

rows.description: Took out of the College Chest
rows.notes: Clear '27 11 6' in the amount columns; description is unambiguous.

rows.description: House Fees from Mr. Savande, Dean
rows.notes: Numeric '13 10 0' is clear. The surname 'Savande' (or similar) is moderately uncertain due to handwriting; 'Dean' is clear. Transcribed the name as it appears.
Bottom-of-section subtotal reads '827 3 8 3/4' with a clear three-quarters mark after pence. Numeric alignment and underline indicate a subtotal; fraction is legible as 3/4.

rows.description: Recepta pro hoc anno & pro debitis anni præcedentis
rows.notes: Final Latin summary line with amount '1575 6 00' clearly written in the pounds/shillings/pence columns. Description is readable in Latin; amount alignment is unambiguous.
